title: quadlet-generator produces MemoryReservation key triggering "Unknown key" warning in systemd 259
body:
### Issue Description

When using Podman Quadlet to generate a service from a `.container` file, the quadlet-generator injects `MemoryReservation=64M` into the `[Service]` section of the generated unit file. Although `MemoryReservation` is a valid `systemd` directive (an alias for `MemoryLow`), it triggers a persistent `"Unknown key"` warning in my user-instance `systemd` logs.

```
Log output: /run/user/1000/systemd/generator/immich-public-proxy.service:32: Unknown key 'MemoryReservation' in section [Service], ignoring.
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Create a `.container` file (e.g., `immich-public-proxy.container`) in `~/.config/containers/systemd/`.

Include the following in the [Service] section:

```
[Service]
MemoryReservation=64M
```
Run `systemctl --user daemon-reload`.

Observe the warning in the journal: `journalctl --user -u immich-public-proxy.service`.

I have only seen it so far with immich-public-proxy,  https://github.com/alangrainger/immich-public-proxy


### Describe the results you received

The container service starts and runs as expected, but every time the unit is loaded or reloaded, systemd emits the following warning:
```
Unknown key 'MemoryReservation' in section [Service], ignoring.
```

### Describe the results you expected

The service should load without log warnings. Given that `MemoryReservation` is technically an alias for `MemoryLow`, it would be ideal if the Quadlet generator either utilized the modern `MemoryLow` key or verified compatibility with the user-instance `systemd` to avoid this log noise.

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

I have reviewed the release notes for versions 5.7.0 through 5.8.3 and the issue persists/is not addressed in the changelogs.

```
Host OS: Ubuntu 26.04
systemd version: 259.5-0ubuntu3 (verified via systemctl --user --version)
```

### Additional information

I initially encountered this while migrating an Immich-Public-Proxy Docker Compose setup to Podman Quadlet. Manually editing the generated file in `/run/user/1000/systemd/generator/` to use `MemoryLow=64M` instead of `MemoryReservation=64M` successfully resolves the warning, which confirms that the underlying `systemd` instance recognizes `MemoryLow` but is rejecting the `MemoryReservation` alias for this specific user-instance configuration.

I realize that this is more of a cosmetic issue than anything else.  Thank you.

comments.body:
In the first paragraph you wrote:
> the quadlet-generator injects MemoryReservation=64M into the [Service] section of the generated unit file

But, in the steps you wrote:
> Include the following in the [Service] section:

So, it's unclear if you are saying that Quadlet adds this line on its own or just copies what you added.

I've looked at the code and run some examples. This line was not added by Quadlet unless I had it in my `.container` file. So, it seems to be the former. It is added because you add it. If that's the case, and the correct key is `MemoryLow` why not just use it?

BTW from what I can tell, `MemoryReservation` is a cgroupv1 parameter while Ubuntu 26.04 uses cgroupv2 by default which no longer supports this value. So, maybe its alias functionality no longer exists. In any case, it does not seem like a Quadlet issue.

Oh shoot. Now I feel stupid. This is 100 percent something I did. My
computer was crashing and this was the last container I spun up before the
crashes started so I created a debug container that hard logged to my nas
in case of crash.  I thought I copied over the .container to a podman run
command but I obviously blew the clock syntax but not enough to have it not
run.

I couldn't figure out for the life of me where that came from, but what you
said made it all clear!

Ugh.

I'll close the issue. This was an error by the unit between the chair and
keyboard.

Thanks for being so gentle, Ygal.

I'll close it.
